name: Auditing
description:
**UC San Diego Data & Integration Services, ITS**
  - **Auditing API** Features the REST services for developers to POST audit records through the UC San Diego API Manager, which further proxies all records to a supporting cloud web service for permanent storage.
  
    Multiple query parameters are allowed for custom filtering on most fields. Additionally, custom endpoints (meta-data) have been  developed to selectively find and extract specific ranges of in-use data useful for standardizing your teams audits.
  
    **1** - Includes subscription security for all records. Queries return only records associated with the subscribed application that posted the record. Enhanced security allows for management of other owned applications, and can be setup upon request...

    **2** - Fully relational, one Constituent may own an unlimited number of Audit records. One Audit record may have unlimited number of Audit Details. One Audit record may be associated with multiple Identity records.

    **3** - Automatically posts all JWT information as a detail record. Automatically stamps current time and date for all incoming posts. Adds JWT identity information to Identity record automatically.

info.title: Auditing
info.description: U.C. San Diego, Auditing API Service

get.summary: Get all the data values of a student,  including name preference data values, using the given student ID.
get.description: A valid student ID must be provided in the URL. The data values associated with name preferences will be automatically saved based on the student's declaration of a lived name. These data values will be included into the final output. The API will return a JSON array of objects, where each object contains a specific data value record for the given student ID.

get.summary: Get a data value using the given student ID and data code.
get.description: A valid student ID and data code must be provided in the URL. The data values associated with name preferences will be automatically saved based on the student's declaration of a lived name. The API will return a JSON array containing a single object, which consists of the data value for the given student ID and data code.

post.summary: Create a data value record for the given student ID and data code.
post.description: A valid student ID must be provided in the URL. The data code, value, and user code should be included in the payload of the POST request. After successfully creating the data value record, the API will return a JSON object containing the details of the new record.

put.summary: Update a data value record for the given student ID and data code.
put.description: A valid student ID must be provided in the URL. The data code, value, and user code should be included in the payload of the PUT request. After successfully updating the data value record, the API will return a JSON object containing the details of the updated record.

delete.summary: Delete a data value record for the given student ID and data code.
delete.description: A valid student ID and data code must be provided in the URL. After successfully deleting the data value record, the API will return the student ID.

info.description: Validates one or more POETAF strings.

tags.name: Validate POETAF
tags.description:
Validates one or more POETAF strings. Checks to make sure that each segment value is valid. Checks to make sure the combination of segments passes OFC PPM Transaction Controls.

There are no parameters for this API, the data is passed as a JSON array in the body. Please see POST examples below.

Required fields

* BusinessUnitName - 'UCSD CAMPUS', 'UCSD MEDICAL CENTER' or 'UCSD PHYSICIANS GROUP'
* OrigTransactionReference (must be unique per batch)
* ExpenditureItemDate - YYYY-MM-DD
* Quantity - Number
* TransactionCurrency - 'USD'
* UnmatchedNegativeTxnFlag - required when Quantity is negative
* ContractNumber - only required for Sponsored Projects
* one of - TaskID, TaskName, TaskNumber
* one of - ProjectID, ProjectName, ProjectNumber
* one of - ExpenditureTypeID, ExpenditureTypeName
* one of - DocumentEntryID, DocumentEntryCode, DocumentEntryName
* one of - DocumentID, DocumentCode, DocumentName
* one of - TransactionSourceID, TransactionSourceCode, SourceName
* one of - ExpenditureOrganizationName, PersonName - req for nonlabor transactions; reference MCIBuilder 'ExpenditureOrganization'

All other fields are ignored / not validated:

* BusinessUnitId
* MyNewField

Known issues

* response for single request is an object, but an array for multple request
* If sending an array of POETAF values to validate, send < 500 at a time.

References

The OFC functions that the validator calls:
COA: Oracle AccountCombinationValidationService

POETAF: Oracle ProjectMiscellaneousTransactionService

post.summary: Add a roster log entry
post.description: Add an entry to the roster log table. The most recent entry for a student in the course the sectionId belongs to determines the current status for the student in the class: Their section ID and whether their allocation is active or expired. When an entry is appended, if one or more current, active, and enabled courses correspond to the `sectionId`, and if the `studentId` specifies a valid student, an allocation will be created for that student in those courses.

200.description: OK - Allocations created for all associated courses

206.description: Roster log entry created, but some or all allocations had errors. Allocations are created automatically if an account for the requested studentId exists; if the course is available for Concurrent Enrollment students to self-add; if it is set with isActive=y and status=on; and if its only allocations are Canvas (facility name 'ted') and/or Podcasting. The 'allocations' field will contain a list of entries with the potential allocation, and a message indicating if it was added ('success'), or a reason why it was not added.

400.description: Bad Request. Typically indicates that an object could not be decoded from the body

404.description: Not Found - the username is not found. (No error will occur if the sectionId does not exist or is not mapped to a class)

info.title: TSS-ScheduleOfClasses
info.description: API to get schedule of class.

tags.name: ScheduleOfClasses
tags.description:
API to provide read-only access to the authoritative schedule of classes, including modules (courses), events (sections), meetings, instructors, and related academic attributes. The API enables consuming systems to retrieve a complete and consistent view of class offerings for registration, advising, scheduling display, and reporting.
This API is a read-only reference API and supports OData-style query capabilities for flexible and performant data retrieval. Please read the [TSS API usage doc](https://ucsdcollab.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SISCORE/pages/2892726466/TSS+core+API+user+guide) in confluence before using this API.

get.summary: Get Schedule of Classes
get.description:
Retrieve the schedule of classes.

**Example to retrieve modules by module Id and term along with events and meetings:**
```
/ScheduleOfClasses?$filter=moduleId eq '00001000' and academicYear eq '2025' and termCode eq 'FA'&$expand=crosslist,coschedule,appraisalTemplate,equivalentModules,instructionTypes,events($expand=instructors,meetings,additionalMeetings,crossenrollment),majorRestrictions,minorRestrictions,collegeRestrictions,academicLevelRestrictions,classLevelRestrictions,corequisites,prerequisiteTests,prerequisites
```

**Example to retrieve only modules by module Id:**
```
/ScheduleOfClasses?$filter=moduleId eq '00001000' and academicYear eq '2025' and termCode eq 'FA'
```

**Example to retrieve only events for a module Id filter by year and term:**
```
ScheduleOfClasses('52912')/events?$filter=offeringYear eq '2025' and termCode eq 'FA'&$expand=instructors,meetings,meetingSchedule,crossEnrollment
```

name: UtilityAPI
description:
**UC San Diego Middleware & Integration Services, ITS**
  - **Core Auditing API** Features the REST services for developers to POST audit records to the UC San Diego API Manager, which further proxies all records to a supporting cloud web service for permanent storage. Additionaly, custom endpoints are being developed to selectively find and extract specific ranges of Audit records tailored to customers requirements.
  
    **1** - All Core Audit Detail records associated with the Audit are always returned
    
    **2** - All Audits for a specific Identity ID can be returned (additional filtering is also possible)
    
    **3** - More...
